basic_info.title: Metal Gear Solid
basic_info.review: Metal Gear Solid is a groundbreaking stealth game that revolutionized the genre with its cinematic presentation, complex narrative, and innovative gameplay mechanics including the Codec communication system and memory card-reading boss battles.

alternative_names: メタルギアソリッド (Metal Gear Solid)

platforms.name: PlayStation 4/5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch, Windows (Steam)

description.synopsis: Solid Snake infiltrates Shadow Moses nuclear facility in Alaska to stop the terrorist group FOXHOUND from launching a nuclear weapon using Metal Gear REX, while uncovering a conspiracy involving genetic engineering and clone warfare.
key_features: Pioneered cinematic cutscenes rendered with in-game engine
Codec radio communication system for storytelling
Psycho Mantis boss battle reading memory card
Multiple difficulty levels including Very Easy mode (Integral)
description.long_description: Metal Gear Solid, directed by Hideo Kojima and released in 1998 for PlayStation, is widely regarded as one of the greatest video games ever made. It revolutionized stealth gaming by introducing cinematic cutscenes rendered using the game's own engine, creating a film-like experience unprecedented at the time. The game features an innovative Codec communication system that delivers exposition and character development through radio conversations with Snake's support team. Its most famous moment involves the boss Psycho Mantis, who reads the player's memory card to determine which games they've played and uses controller vibration accordingly. The expanded Integral version added VR Missions (300 training challenges), a Very Easy difficulty setting, first-person mode, and additional content. The game sold over 7 million copies worldwide and influenced countless stealth-action games including Splinter Cell, Assassin's Creed, and the rest of the Metal Gear Solid series. It was later remade as Metal Gear Solid: The Twin Snakes for GameCube in 2004 with updated graphics and gameplay mechanics.
legacy_and_impact: Established cinematic presentation standards in video games
Popularized the stealth action genre globally
Introduced innovative storytelling through Codec system
Influenced Splinter Cell, Assassin's Creed, and other stealth games
Featured in Smithsonian American Art Museum exhibition 'The Art of Video Games'
Named one of Time magazine's 100 greatest video games of all time
